opponent_lineup.source: Their batting order from our last meeting — Jul 10, 2025 (L 8-10 at Boy Scout Park). GameChanger only logs the opponent pitcher's fielding position; 'Hits to' shows which of our positions their balls actually went to.

rules: Players with the most 2025 errors sit more (rule set Jun 2026): Coulombe (7 E) sits inning 5 over Amann (1 E); Cheever (10 E) sits inning 2 over DiLibero (4 E); nobody sits twice; everyone bats all game.
Marks is the only SS with McArdle out, so he plays short all 7. Klatt is the only pitcher.
Coulombe (C/2B utility) rovers into each rested spot; Cheever backfills short field, catches inning 1, sits inning 2.

grid.why: A .565 OBP and exactly one strikeout all season. The leadoff job is "don't make the first out," and Ryan treats outs as a personal insult.
Team-best 2.391 OPS (.689, 7 HR) bats second so the biggest bat comes up with Ryan already standing on base. Yes, he also writes the lineup. The math just happens to agree with him.
Hit .524 with zero walks and zero strikeouts in 63 trips — physically incapable of not putting the ball in play. The 3-hole keeps the line moving.
14 HR and 53 RBI, team highs in both. He bats cleanup and hosts the tailgate — the man simply cleans up wherever he goes.
Hit .473 with 4 triples — still hits it deep and, more impressively, still agrees to run all the way to third.
.476 with gap power (7 doubles) keeps the middle of the order moving. Also our only pitcher, and you keep your only pitcher happy.
Quietly hit .469 in part-time duty and fielded a perfect 1.000. The 7-hole is the "we see you, Matt" slot — keep this up and he's coming for the top six.
Ignore the .394 average — the .652 SLG (8 doubles, 3 HR) is the real story. Boom-or-bust pop to restart a rally, batting where a man playing all 7 innings at short can pace his legs.
Team-high 6 walks in a league where taking a pitch is considered rude. The 9-spot is the second leadoff — patience starts the bottom-third rally.
New guy, zero 2025 data — the algorithm just shrugged. Tonight is the audition: hit, and the spreadsheet learns your name fast.
Hit .313 and bats last with one job: turn the lineup over. Anything Corey starts, the top of the order finishes.
